109228.explanation: D66 Amstelveen wants to involve residents through city discussions, surveys, and citizen forums. Consultative referendums can help, but D66 is cautious about binding referendums because local issues are often complex and require careful consideration.

109229.explanation: D66 sees the management of facilities such as sports halls and public green spaces as a core task of the municipality, due to continuity and expertise. At the same time, we encourage neighborhood initiatives and residents who want to participate or help out, in addition to and in cooperation with the municipality.

109231.explanation: D66 believes that cafés and restaurants should in principle be able to determine their own opening hours. However, this should not lead to nuisance for local residents. With clear rules and good enforcement, we ensure a balance between lively hospitality and livable neighborhoods.

109232.explanation: D66 Amstelveen believes municipalities are responsible for the reception of people fleeing war and persecution. As long as the national government does not have the asylum chain in order, Amstelveen does not shy away from a fair and humane reception and we meet the targets of the Distribution Act.

109233.explanation: D66 finds it undesirable that rejected asylum seekers end up on the street causing possible nuisance or insecurity. People without the right to stay must return humanely and quickly. Reception is not a structural solution and is therefore only desirable in exceptional cases.

109234.explanation: D66 does not want to ban wood burning but also does not want to encourage it. It is harmful to health and can cause nuisance. We focus on education, cleaner alternatives, subsidies for removing fireplaces, and clear rules during orange or red air quality codes.

109236.explanation: Fireworks and ringing in the new year together are a tradition for many people. D66 Amstelveen wants to preserve that experience and supports municipality-organized fireworks shows in the neighborhoods, or alternatives like a drone show. This way we celebrate together, festively and safely.

109237.explanation: D66 finds it responsible that collecting bulky waste requires a contribution. Collecting and processing bulky waste costs money, and that should be paid fairly. Fee waivers are possible for people with low incomes. This keeps bulky waste collection accessible and encourages careful use.

109238.explanation: D66 believes festivals and parties are valuable for a lively city and wants to continue providing space for them. This must not come at the expense of health and residential comfort. Therefore, no generic tightening of noise standards, but clear frameworks regarding the number of festivals, tailored solutions, and enforcement.

109240.explanation: D66 believes sport should be accessible to everyone. That is why we invest in enriched school days, outdoor sports, and good social assistance arrangements. People receiving social assistance benefits get help with sports fees. This works well and does not need to be expanded.

109243.explanation: D66 believes every child should be able to develop broadly. Therefore, we invest in enriched school days with art, culture, and music for all children. Through music lessons at school and cooperation with Platform C, we enable participation and keep music education accessible.

109247.explanation: D66 wants to protect privacy and allow camera surveillance only in cases of serious nuisance and under strict conditions. We prefer other visible safety measures such as public enforcement officers, neighborhood police officers, and improved street lighting. This way, safety and freedom go hand in hand.

109249.explanation: The safety of enforcement officers is important. D66 is cautious about arming public enforcement officers. De-escalation, good training, and cooperation with the police are paramount. Only if risks demonstrably increase can an expansion of equipment be carefully considered.

109255.explanation: D66 believes people should be able to participate, but opposes a mandatory counter-performance in social assistance. Research shows that coercion does not work. We choose trust, education, tailored approaches, and cooperation with employers, with room for voluntary commitment where appropriate.

109256.explanation: D66 considers fee waivers justified for residents who demonstrably cannot pay their municipal taxes. This requires an individual assessment: no generic policy, but tailored solutions. This prevents debts and keeps facilities affordable for everyone.

109259.explanation: D66 is not in favor of an unconditional basic income for everyone. Work should pay off and public money should go to those who need it. However, D66 advocates simplifying the system locally and nationally, reducing rules, and increasing trust in people.

109260.explanation: D66 expects growth in the number of electric cars and wants the number of charging points to grow accordingly. We encourage smart charging, such as home and neighborhood charging solutions, and designate parking spots as charging points where necessary. We also ensure fair distribution of public space.

109265.explanation: D66 supports parking permits where parking pressure is demonstrably too high. Not generically, but based on measurements. This keeps neighborhoods livable and accessible. Revenues are invested in sustainable mobility and good alternatives such as shared cars and (cargo) bikes.

109266.explanation: D66 believes care should be tailored to the individual. People should be able to choose a healthcare provider that fits their religion, culture, or beliefs, provided quality and professionalism are guaranteed. With tailored and culturally sensitive care, we strengthen trust and good care for everyone.

109271.explanation: Amstelveen has a strong local economy with business parks that are largely full. Large-scale expansion is only possible at the Amstelveen Zuid Business Park (BTAZ). D66 opts for sustainable growth there, with attention to accessibility and quality of life for the surroundings.

109279.explanation: D66 wants a regulated coffeeshop in Amstelveen, at a central and safe location. As a normal facility, comparable to a café. Regulation increases safety, reduces illegal trade, and protects public health through control and education.

109280.explanation: D66 wants to build where possible and chooses mixed neighborhoods. In new construction, we strive for a balanced mix: about one-third social housing, one-third mid-range, and a maximum of one-third expensive. This way we build for young people, seniors, and families and prevent one-sided neighborhoods.

109281.explanation: D66 takes the housing shortage seriously and is open to more high-rise buildings in carefully chosen locations, such as near public transport hubs and neighborhood centers. This saves green space and allows us to realize more affordable housing for different groups.

109282.explanation: D66 opposes a general ban on subdividing homes. Shared housing can help with the housing shortage, especially for young people and starters. We do set clear conditions to prevent nuisance and protect livability in neighborhoods. Tailored solutions per neighborhood are paramount.

109284.explanation: D66 bases parking policy on measured parking pressure. Resident permits are cost-covering; street parking is mainly for visitors. We only lower rates if it demonstrably contributes to livability and accessibility, for example with low parking pressure or adverse effects on shops and residents.

109286.explanation: D66 wants an accessible and lively City Center. We give more space to walking, cycling, and public transport, but also keep the center accessible by car. With smart design, we ensure safety, greenery, and social interaction without losing accessibility.

109287.explanation: D66 considers enforcement important for traffic safety. Bus gates exist for a reason. Good signage and clear information are essential. Warnings can be appropriate in case of mistakes, but those who deliberately break rules must expect a fine.

109288.explanation: For D66 Amstelveen, the health and livability of residents come first. To reduce noise nuisance and air pollution, we advocate at the national level for fewer takeoff and landing movements over the municipality, until air traffic is demonstrably quieter and cleaner.

109289.explanation: D66 wants to structurally tackle energy poverty. We help households by making homes more sustainable and permanently reducing energy bills, through energy fixers and agreements with housing corporations. This way, we provide targeted support that truly helps in the long term.

109228.explanation: Decisions require careful consideration and protection of minorities. We opt for involvement through citizen panels, neighborhood discussions, and participation that connects.

109229.explanation: Residents know their neighborhood and can work with the municipality to ensure management that fits the area. This strengthens ownership and involvement, but remains complementary to professional responsibility.

109231.explanation: Liveliness is valuable, but so is livability. Clear rules protect residents and ensure balance.

109232.explanation: Refugees deserve protection. Shelter must be humane, small-scale, and well embedded in society.

109233.explanation: No one should end up on the street. Care, humanity, and guidance remain necessary, even after procedures are completed.

109234.explanation: Clean air and health take precedence over individual freedom. Nuisance and health risks call for clear limits.

109236.explanation: Depending on support. Safety, solidarity, and calm for people and animals are guiding principles.

109237.explanation: Good waste facilities prevent litter and inequality and keep neighborhoods clean and livable. Asking for a small personal contribution prevents many trips and encourages targeted use.

109238.explanation: Parties are allowed, but with respect for residents. Tailored solutions per location and time are necessary.

109240.explanation: Sport connects and promotes health. Money should not be a barrier to participation.

109243.explanation: Culture shapes and connects. Every child deserves the chance to discover talent and play together.

109247.explanation: Safety starts with proximity. Cameras only temporarily and focused; no surveillance state.

109249.explanation: Community officers are for proximity and de-escalation. Arming them does not fit.

109255.explanation: Participation according to ability is valuable, but never compulsory or punishable.

109256.explanation: Debt forgiveness prevents problematic debts among low incomes.

109259.explanation: Provided it is well researched what works within social security and participation.

109260.explanation: Charging infrastructure makes clean transport more accessible for everyone.

109265.explanation: Only where pressure is high and in consultation with residents.

109266.explanation: Care must fit who people are. Freedom and dignity come first.

109271.explanation: Only if it is sustainable and fits with space, nature, and employment.

109279.explanation: We want to counteract normalization of drug use and protect youth.

109280.explanation: We choose 30% social, 40% mid-range, and 30% expensive housing for mixed neighborhoods and good flow.

109281.explanation: There is a large building task and lack of space. In some places building upwards can help. At the same time, we protect the balance: high residential towers only where they fit with facilities and the scale of the environment.

109282.explanation: Per-room rental can contribute to affordable housing, especially for young people and starters. At the same time, we opt for tailored solutions with attention to livability in neighborhoods.

109284.explanation: Paid parking is not a goal in itself. It is about livable neighborhoods and fair choices in mobility. Sometimes price steering is necessary, sometimes not. Look per neighborhood what is needed and offer real alternatives: good public transport, safe cycling routes, and shared mobility.

109286.explanation: A car-free center gives space to people: more safety, greenery, and social interaction. That only works with good accessibility at the edges, strong public transport, and space for those truly dependent on cars. This way the center becomes livable and welcoming.

109287.explanation: Rules must be clear and enforceable. The bus gates are known and essential for safety and flow. Those who deliberately violate should be fined.

109288.explanation: Fewer flight movements mean less noise nuisance, emissions, and sleep disturbance. Also, Schiphol limits housing construction space in and around Amstelveen. Health, quality of life, and the housing shortage call for clear limits on aviation.

109289.explanation: No one should be left out in the cold. Extra support helps prevent debts and stress. At the same time, we invest in support for sustainability for people with small budgets, so energy costs go down structurally and the energy transition is fair for everyone.

109228.explanation: The VVD values representative democracy and good participation, but prefers consultation and input over referenda as a decision-making tool.

109229.explanation: The VVD welcomes management by residents if quality, safety, and continuity are guaranteed. It must not be a disguised cut: the municipality remains responsible for good agreements and appropriate support.

109231.explanation: Entrepreneurs should largely determine their own opening hours. Only in case of demonstrable nuisance can tailored restrictions be necessary.

109232.explanation: The VVD is not in favor of asylum reception in Amstelveen, but the municipality must comply with national agreements and task assignments. Nationally, the VVD wants to limit inflow, so the need for reception locations decreases.

109233.explanation: For those whose procedures are concluded, return applies. The municipality must follow national policy and not maintain separate local shelters for people without residence rights.

109234.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves may remain, but nuisance and health must be taken into account. Education and enforcement in case of excesses are important.

109236.explanation: A central show can be nice, but a “must” creates fixed costs. VVD chooses to be cautious with new structural expenditures, allowing room for private initiatives.

109237.explanation: The VVD supports the principle that the polluter pays. Free collection can lead to increased use; low, fair rates are better than completely free.

109238.explanation: The VVD wants a vibrant nightlife. Existing standards should limit nuisance, but further tightening makes events and hospitality less attractive.

109240.explanation: Sport for children from low-income families may be supported, but a general obligation to fully reimburse all contributions goes too far for the VVD.

109243.explanation: The VVD encourages music and cultural education, but no unconditional rights for all children. Prefer projects and targeted support rather than a generic subsidy scheme.

109247.explanation: Camera surveillance at nuisance locations and on access roads increases the feeling of security and helps track crime, within clear privacy frameworks.

109249.explanation: Enforcement officers must be able to protect themselves safely. A baton can, with proper training and clear deployment criteria, contribute to their safety and authority.

109255.explanation: Those receiving benefits who can work must provide a social contribution where possible. This promotes participation and personal responsibility.

109256.explanation: For residents with very low incomes, waiver of fees may be necessary. The VVD wants to prevent abuse, but not completely stop support for genuine low-income individuals.

109259.explanation: The VVD opposes a basic income. It undermines incentives to work and is financially unsustainable. Work and targeted support are better.

109260.explanation: The VVD wants to facilitate electric driving, but without disadvantaging regular parkers. Expansion of charging points should be demand-driven per neighborhood, considering enough regular parking spaces.

109265.explanation: Permit parking is only appropriate where there is demonstrably high parking pressure. The VVD opposes further rollout as standard, preferring tailored solutions per neighborhood.

109266.explanation: Personal preference is important, but an absolute right to “always be able to choose” can be unfeasible. The VVD wants freedom of choice where reasonably possible.

109271.explanation: Entrepreneurs and employment are important. Expansion is possible, provided it is carefully integrated and considers the living environment and accessibility.

109279.explanation: The VVD is cautious about new coffeeshops due to safety and livability concerns. First, maintain control over existing soft drug issues.

109280.explanation: Amstelveen already has about one-third social rental housing, more than the national average. The VVD primarily wants to add mid-range rent and owner-occupied housing. A norm of 50% social rental in new construction is too one-sided and hinders mixed, financially feasible projects.

109281.explanation: Amstelveen must retain its green, spacious character. The VVD generally wants a maximum of five floors, with exceptions in a few places only.

109282.explanation: The VVD wants livable, quiet neighborhoods and opposes excessive subdivision into multiple rooms that causes nuisance but is open to room rental to, for example, students if this fits well in the environment and stays within clear legal and municipal frameworks.

109284.explanation: Parking costs for residents must remain limited. Where possible, the VVD wants to reduce rates or at least prevent further increases.

109286.explanation: The center must remain easily accessible for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs. The VVD does not want to completely ban cars but wants smart regulation.

109287.explanation: Bus gates are for public transport priority and safety. The VVD wants clear rules and effective enforcement; a fine is part of that.

109288.explanation: Schiphol is important for the economy, but the nuisance for Amstelveen residents is too great. The VVD wants quieter routes and fewer (night) flights over the municipality and asks the national government for measures that better balance the economy and quality of life.

109289.explanation: People with payment problems should receive targeted help, but additional funds must fit within the budget and be linked to effective measures.

109228.explanation: More participation for residents. Indicate the minimum required percentage of votes (electoral threshold) that applies. Also, provide a clear and fair description of pros and cons from various independent perspectives.

109229.explanation: Only if this improves efficiency and leads to a cleaner and greener city or if there is a threat of closure of a sports hall.

109231.explanation: As long as no nuisance arises for the residential environment.

109232.explanation: The already scarce space can only be used once. Especially with one of the highest waiting times in the region.

109233.explanation: Rejected also means deported. No extra costs should be incurred.

109234.explanation: Population density must be taken into account. Especially in damp and windless weather, wood stoves pose a problem mainly for the elderly and residents with respiratory problems.

109236.explanation: The 50PLUS program supports the idea of a municipal fireworks show, but not as a general obligation.

109237.explanation: The 50PLUS program does include passages about a fair waste fee (‘charge according to household size’) and circular economy. The polluter pays accordingly.

109238.explanation: 50PLUS Amstelveen advocates clear noise standards that take into account neighbors. Both people and animals.

109240.explanation: Not necessarily. Amstelveen already has a generous allowance for low-income people up to 150% of the minimum wage.

109243.explanation: 50PLUS Amstelveen supports accessible cultural education for all children in Amstelveen and is willing to provide financial support for this.

109247.explanation: Safety in Amstelveen and beyond is a top priority for 50PLUS Amstelveen. We support targeted camera surveillance where it demonstrably helps.

109249.explanation: Arming enforcement officers is regulated nationally, not locally. Municipalities cannot decide this independently.

109255.explanation: A mandatory societal contribution for social assistance is not part of our program. We want to focus on basic security, not on obligations for social assistance recipients.

109256.explanation: We support a review of the waiver policy. A preliminary analysis must determine the best direction for this.

109259.explanation: 50PLUS Amstelveen is not convinced of the usefulness of a basic income.

109260.explanation: Expansion of charging spots-provided it does not burden the neighborhood-can happen in the future when the rise of rechargeable cars explicitly demands it.

109265.explanation: 50PLUS Amstelveen is only willing to support paid parking if the parking pressure is demonstrably too high and is also experienced as a problem by the residents themselves.

109266.explanation: It is nice if care can be provided that matches someone's background where this is practically possible. However, the primary concern is providing the right and timely care.

109271.explanation: Space in Amstelveen is very scarce. Existing business parks are being converted into residential locations. Therefore, expanding the net area of business parks is not possible.

109279.explanation: Amstelveen already offers sufficient opportunities for this target group.

109280.explanation: Not as a standard due to affordability. For individual projects, 50PLUS Amstelveen even advocates 100% allocation if it concerns targeted assignment to our youth and elderly. Especially since we have one of the longest waiting times in the region.

109281.explanation: 50PLUS Amstelveen supports appropriate housing construction but opposes mass construction of tower blocks or similar residential buildings.

109282.explanation: By exception, a permit can allow deviation from this.

109284.explanation: Paid parking only if it is cost-covering and serves as a solution to a confirmed problem.

109286.explanation: Car-free is not necessary; traffic must be properly managed. The center connects closely to the highway, which can help limit nuisance.

109287.explanation: Enforcement should be preventive; if a warning has an effect, that is a fine idea.

109288.explanation: Health takes precedence over economic interests. However, reduction is not a goal in itself. Support is given for fewer night flights of transport aircraft and for quieter and cleaner planes. Additionally, better distribution through the use of Lelystad Airport.

109289.explanation: Not necessarily. Determining whether the municipality gives people extra money should depend on many more factors than just their inability to pay the energy bill. A national reduction of the energy tax is more appropriate.

109228.explanation: But, Actief Voor Amstelveen prefers the introduction of a Citizens' Initiative, allowing our residents to bring agenda items to the municipal council themselves, so they can be seen and heard.

109229.explanation: Facilitating sports is a profession, as is hospitality operation. Therefore, hospitality entrepreneurs should be able to apply for a lease agreement. For outdoor area management-green maintenance & litter removal-volunteers could be deployed with a volunteer allowance.

109231.explanation: Royal Horeca Netherlands Amstelland asked this question to its members, and the answer is: don’t do it. Also, the current regulations are adequate and desirable for the living environment near hospitality businesses.

109232.explanation: Do not shift failing national asylum policy onto municipalities. Of course, there must be shelter for people fleeing war zones, but then in temporary, modest accommodations and not in our scarce social rental homes, for which Amstelveen residents have been on the waiting list for over 15 years.

109233.explanation: There is no place in Amstelveen for rejected asylum seekers, economic asylum seekers (fortune seekers), and nuisance-causing asylum seekers. These should not even be able to reach Amstelveen.

109234.explanation: Do not intervene in private circumstances or prohibit; do not punish but reward. So let the installation be inspected free of charge; the owner then modifies the installation at their own expense (possibly with a small subsidy). However, providers should be engaged about a quality mark for newly sold installations.

109236.explanation: Firecrackers have been legally banned for years; only legally obtained fireworks were allowed. Enforcement was and remains the biggest problem and a fireworks show will not solve it. Where would you safely hold such a large-scale event in Amstelveen and where would you find skilled professionals for igniting it?

109237.explanation: Certainly indispensable, especially for elderly who cannot go to the waste station themselves. Also, regular collection is necessary to prevent street littering. However, stricter enforcement is needed against misuse and it should only be free for Amstelveen residents and entrepreneurs.

109238.explanation: Amstelveen could use a party, and certainly our youth look forward to it. Relatively few complaints are received, and organizers already spend a fortune on safety and environmental requirements. So more regulations would discourage organizations. However, complaints should continue to be monitored.

109240.explanation: Choices must be made both at the municipality and at home. Currently, there are already several schemes for the minimum income group to make sports accessible to as many people as possible. For middle incomes there are none, and paying contributions for the children is not always possible there either.

109243.explanation: The municipality is not an ATM and must make choices. There is already plenty of financial support for providers of music lessons (Platform C). Schools might possibly need to be more involved.

109247.explanation: If you have nothing to hide, camera surveillance is not a problem at all and contributes to greater safety. Regarding privacy, more videos are made and shared with the newest phones than the municipality's cameras could ever capture.

109249.explanation: People from inside and outside Amstelveen are increasingly short-tempered and have less respect for our laws, regulations, and social manners. Here, BOA's (special enforcement officers), where necessary and with clear agreements, could possibly maintain order with a baton. However, this must be well monitored.

109255.explanation: If health or physical problems do not stand in the way, it would be a good thing, also for reintegration of welfare recipients, to get back into the rhythm of a work process. This will help with self-esteem, meaning in life, and possibly lead to employment.

109256.explanation: What is a low income? For someone with benefits easily determined, but the silent poverty among the middle groups is difficult to understand due to false shame, and they do pay off their debts and taxes.

109259.explanation: Work must pay off! There is already a lot of financial support for the minimum income group, which is good. But if health and physical condition allow, there must still be a financial incentive to contribute to society. Middle incomes often also have difficulty making ends meet.

109260.explanation: We oppose this because these spots probably come at the expense of the current supply. If new construction provides space for this, fine; also consider exorbitant prices for charging stations providers and do not grant permits in that case.

109265.explanation: Residents of neighborhoods must be much more involved in this and then the right of the majority counts. Due to a motion by Actief Voor Amstelveen, the first 2 visiting hours are currently free, and the permit must remain affordable.

109266.explanation: Where possible, yes, but this is becoming a luxury problem. Care is becoming unaffordable and there are too few hands at the bedside. For a caregiver, a patient is a patient and everyone will provide the right love and care.

109271.explanation: Amstelveen should not become an industrial city. However, where possible, business parks should be swapped with areas where residential construction is allowed. Furthermore, more residential destinations should be realized on business parks; people then have the choice whether or not to live there. Starters will be less critical in these areas.

109279.explanation: National legislation allows for the use and sale of cannabis products. So why not in Amstelveen, where now scooter couriers operate uncontrolled trade? Naturally, with strict supervision and not within a radius of 1000 meters near schools or residences.

109280.explanation: But who will build these then? Project developers won’t because they cannot break even. A solution is to start a municipal Housing Corporation with capital from the municipality (so cuts are needed) and banks. Then the municipality can also decide who gets these homes, starting with Amstelveen residents.

109281.explanation: With our scarce land and high demand for housing, we will have to build upwards. Naturally, consideration must be given to neighbors and they should be involved in the plans in a timely manner. Adding floors to some flats would also be an option. Only: building, building - for whom? Amstelveen residents?

109282.explanation: For students, finding a room is a challenge. So, let’s also involve single people in social rental homes, with permission from the Housing Corporation. Renting out an entire house will lead to a “student house” in the street, resulting in nuisance.

109284.explanation: Keep the permit fees for Amstelveen residents at the same level, possibly extra discounts for the elderly. More blue zones near shopping strips, neighborhood centers, and sports facilities. Thanks to the motion by Actief Voor Amstelveen, the first two visiting hours are already free; continue this.

109286.explanation: Residents should have the choice to come to the center by public transport, bicycle, on foot, or car; they don’t need the municipality’s patronizing. Also, for entrepreneurs with very high rents, it is important that the center is easily accessible.

109287.explanation: Before a bus lock (bussluis) is installed, there must be sufficient support from local residents; this was insufficient in Bovenkerk. Of course, everyone must comply with the law once this is realized, but if a long-standing through road is suddenly closed, some adjustment is required.

109288.explanation: Schiphol is very important to 10,000 Amstelveen residents who work there and also of great economic importance. Naturally, Schiphol must comply with all laws and regulations and do everything to limit nuisance, especially from night flights. The opening of Lelystad will help with that.

109289.explanation: Of course, no one should be left out in the cold and we should step in where necessary. But it should not be automatic, as there are residents who carefully monitor their usage (one sets the thermostat at 30 degrees, another at 16 degrees) and pay less through this frugality. Tailored solutions are desirable here.

109228.explanation: As residents, we decide about our own municipality. When an issue is very prominent, a referendum can be a fair and great way to make a decision. This way, we involve people in local democracy. Citizen assemblies and neighborhood budgets sometimes fit better.

109229.explanation: If you give people the trust to build something together in their own neighborhood, beautiful things arise. The municipality should not hinder this but rather encourage it.

109231.explanation: The municipality should not patronize. Entrepreneurs should be able to set their own opening hours. Of course, if a café or restaurant causes nuisance in the neighborhood, the municipality can adjust the opening hours.

109232.explanation: People fleeing from war deserve a safe place. Amstelveen must take responsibility for humane accommodation. We continue to provide shelter, fairly distributed. And from day one, we start integration through language lessons, education, and work.

109233.explanation: We ensure that no one sleeps on the street and that everyone has access to care and food. Those not entitled to asylum must return to their country of origin. The municipality focuses on shelter and integration of people who need protection.

109234.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA chooses health. Other cities have preceded us in opting for clean air. Also in Amstelveen, people with asthma or other respiratory difficulties suffer from fine particulate matter. In an urban area, especially when there is no wind, a wood stove can unfortunately no longer be permitted.

109236.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all celebrate together, with one big fireworks show organized by the municipality. This keeps it safe and allows us to enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together. Without eye injuries, without harassment of emergency responders, and without scared pets.

109237.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other litter on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and gives us cleaner streets.

109238.explanation: Excessive noise harms health and livability. A fun party should be possible, but this can go hand in hand with a healthy living environment and protection of neighbors. The municipality must also set clear limits to nuisance at festivals and parties.

109240.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can participate in a sports club for free.

109243.explanation: Music lessons stimulate talent, creativity, and self-confidence. Children today face enormous pressure, from addictive social media to academic pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons to be accessible not only to children with wealthy parents. Where necessary, we ensure lower prices through subsidies.

109247.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA chooses safety through visible people in the neighborhood, such as community enforcement officers, neighborhood police officers, and youth workers. Trust and presence work better than anonymous surveillance. More cameras alone infringe on privacy and often do not solve problems.

109249.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. Law enforcement officers with weapons already exist: the police. We choose for community enforcement officers who are visible in the neighborhood, who engage with people, and who are trained to reduce tensions. Not to use physical force.

109255.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

109256.explanation: Even people with the lowest incomes must be able to make ends meet. Debt forgiveness prevents debts and poverty. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses security of existence and a strong social safety net. We will not push the most vulnerable Amstelveners into the arms of the debt industry.

109259.explanation: A pilot with a basic income can provide more security of existence and less stress. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to experiment with new forms of income security, based on trust and equal opportunities for everyone.

109260.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA facilitates sustainable choices. We will therefore continue to focus on walking, cycling, and public transport. More charging stations make electric driving easier. This also makes car driving cleaner.

109265.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA chooses an accessible city with more space for living, cycling, walking, and greenery. Permit parking reduces parking pressure and keeps neighborhoods livable. This allows residents to find a spot more easily and better utilizes scarce space.

109266.explanation: Care must be tailored to who someone is. By offering freedom of choice, we ensure respectful and inclusive care. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses customization, equality, and combating discrimination.

109271.explanation: Land in Amstelveen is scarce. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses housing and preservation of green space, not expansion of business parks. We focus on smart use and transformation of existing areas.

109279.explanation: Such a new coffeeshop will only receive a permit for regulated and safe sales. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses realistic policy with clear rules. Nuisance must be prevented and combated. Public health and safety must be better protected.

109280.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants a lot more affordable housing to be built. Of all new construction, we build at least 40% social and 40% mid-range housing. These homes are the most needed. There is no longer room for villas in Amstelveen. Fifty percent social rental housing sounds great, but is not feasible.

109281.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage and action is needed. With high-rise buildings, you can quickly add many homes. We say: build upward near good public transport connections. This way, you build affordable new homes without sacrificing nature and green spaces.

109282.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage, and many people in our municipality live more spaciously than necessary. Therefore, we want to make it easier to rent out individual rooms, as long as they are well maintained and a fair rent is charged. Of course, abuses must be prevented.

109284.explanation: Permit parking sooner or later becomes inevitable due to the spillover effect. Paid parking helps reduce parking pressure and keeps neighborhoods livable. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses fair use of scarce space and encourages walking, cycling, and public transport over extra car use.

109286.explanation: Amstelveen Stadshart and the old village are already largely car-free. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses vibrant centers where walking, cycling, and staying are central. In this way, we make the center healthier and more attractive for everyone.

109287.explanation: Bus gates serve safety and flow of public transport. Those who ignore them endanger others. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses clear rules and consistent enforcement so that gates actually work.

109288.explanation: Planes cause noise nuisance and pollution-most Amstelveners can confirm this. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses health and livability. Amstelveen must work with the national government to reduce flights and better protect residents.

109289.explanation: No one should be left in the cold due to lack of money. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses security of existence and assists people with low energy bills. Additional support prevents debts and energy poverty and ensures that everyone can keep participating.

109228.explanation: In our election program, SP advocates for a binding corrective referendum. We gauge opinions in advance. The council makes a decision, residents have the right to submit this decision to a referendum. We proposed this for parking; unfortunately, a large majority of the council was against it.

109229.explanation: SP has been advocating for years in Amstelveen to give residents the opportunity, if they wish, to take over some management and maintenance tasks from the municipality. Explicitly at the request of residents, not as a budget cut, and the municipality remains owner and director.

109231.explanation: There are no discussions in Amstelveen regarding opening hours. Where desired or nuisance is experienced, a suitable solution should be sought in consultation with the café owner and neighbors.

109232.explanation: Amstelveen has only recently opened a few locations; there is no reason to close them.

109233.explanation: In the past, SP in Amstelveen advocated for Bed, Bath, Bread-a sober shelter for rejected asylum seekers. Nationally, it must be arranged that rejected asylum seekers leave the country. In the municipality, we must prevent people from wandering the streets or disappearing from sight.

109234.explanation: A limited amount of wood burning, for example for a cozy fireplace, should be possible. Large-scale burning to heat the entire house also causes nuisance. Some regulation is wise.

109236.explanation: In the municipal council, SP has previously advocated for a fireworks ban, provided alternatives are offered. A fireworks show is such an alternative.

109237.explanation: Not everyone owns a car; the municipality provides free borrowing of a cargo bike, but this is not always sufficient. There is already an option for low-income residents, which may be expanded, with possible restrictions on type and frequency.

109238.explanation: Festivals bring enjoyment, so these should be allowed in a limited way, while being critical of (noise) nuisance. Enforcement must be strict. Public spaces belong to us all. In residential areas, residents suffer; in places like Amsterdam Forest, it is the nature that is affected. Fun instead of loud is the priority!

109240.explanation: SP has already successfully advocated for support for low-income people in sports and culture and increasing the amounts. Unfortunately, it is not always sufficient for costs. At the very least, membership fees must reasonably be affordable.

109243.explanation: Music is a connecting factor for people. Being able to play yourself or learn to appreciate it is something that should be encouraged at a young age.

109247.explanation: SP prefers human supervision on the street. Cameras offer a false sense of security and at best help with prosecution/conviction afterward.

109249.explanation: Violence provokes violence. The monopoly on violence belongs to the police, who are trained for it.

109255.explanation: If people do socially meaningful work, they should also receive a meaningful wage for it, at least the minimum wage and, if applicable, wages according to collective labor agreements. No forced labor for starvation wages!

109256.explanation: Low-income people are having a hard time; there is a food bank in Amstelveen for a reason. Let's ensure the food bank is no longer needed before we start talking about stopping debt forgiveness.

109259.explanation: It is good to look for alternatives to minimum income policies and the whole benefits circus.

109260.explanation: There must be enough parking spaces for both electric and non-electric cars. More charging options, but not necessarily exclusivity...

109265.explanation: Parking permits should only be introduced if two conditions are met: parking pressure is high AND the majority of neighborhood residents consider it necessary. The SP advocated for a referendum on this and a binding support measurement; unfortunately, neither topic made it (yet).

109266.explanation: People who need care are in a dependent position, often with a significant invasion of privacy. This is necessary, and precisely then it is important to be helped in accordance with your own beliefs, so there must be freedom of choice.

109271.explanation: There are several designated locations for business parks where space is still available. Housing construction has priority. Fortunately, office vacancy is declining; let's not create new vacancy.

109279.explanation: There is currently no coffeeshop in Amstelveen. It is hypocritical to refer to Amsterdam. Furthermore, home sales do take place, which we can reduce with this.

109280.explanation: In our election program, the SP advocates for 40%. To reduce waiting lists in the short term, 50% helps with new construction. This way, we catch up faster on the backlog caused by virtually no social rental housing being built from 2010 to 2020.

109281.explanation: The entire municipal council unanimously supported the high-rise vision. There are places where high-rise is possible, and without high-rise we cannot meet the housing task. But with policy and not everywhere immediately to the maximum (50 meters).

109282.explanation: Renting out individual rooms is a profit model to extract more money from a home and thus exploit the housing crisis.

109284.explanation: Parking permits in Amstelveen are relatively cheap and are based on cost coverage, not making a profit. There is also a low visitor rate in most neighborhoods for those who specifically visit a resident. Even the street rates for other visitors are reasonable outside the Stadshart area.

109286.explanation: The center is already low-traffic and so small that a car-free Amstelveen is a non-issue. At most, you would increase parking pressure in the nearby residential neighborhoods...

109287.explanation: It is always possible to look for possible improvements in markings. Those who knowingly run a red light receive a fine, so those who knowingly ignore the bus gate also do...

109288.explanation: The SP also advocates national shrinkage and has repeatedly called for a more active role of the municipality in the Schiphol dossier.

109289.explanation: Energy poverty exists and must be combated.

109228.explanation: The residents of Amstelveen should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

109229.explanation: Yes, it is allowed, but we must organize it well to ensure quality.

109233.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers must be helped with return, so no official shelter. We will remain humane and provide emergency aid to those who need it or are homeless.

109228.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen is basically not against a referendum. However, we believe it can only be supported with certainty if the majority of parties agree. Ultimately, everyone must look in the same direction.

109229.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen disagrees here. For management, people with expertise are needed, and the same goes for professionals who maintain greenery and public spaces. However, we do support a tolerant policy if people want to create their own garden or planting area.

109231.explanation: It depends on the location. Cafes and restaurants in a shopping center depend on the rules of the shopping center's manager. Outside of that, people may decide their own opening hours. Goed voor Amstelveen believes politics should not interfere with opening hours.

109232.explanation: The municipality of Amstelveen is bound by national laws and regulations regarding asylum reception. We believe municipalities themselves should decide whether or not asylum reception should be present.

109233.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers should fall under the national government. Moreover, it is a matter of customization what the municipality wants or does not want to facilitate.

109234.explanation: Amstelveen deals with air pollution from Tata Steel, Schiphol, and highways; the few open fireplaces and wood stoves are not proportional. Goed voor Amstelveen values sustainability highly, but we believe balance is very important.

109236.explanation: The municipality is free to organize a fireworks/laser show. Goed voor Amstelveen believes it will attract people and possibly reduce vandalism. "Must" is not the right word. It depends on budget and resources.

109237.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen believes the municipality incurs costs for disposing of bulky waste. Our depot is nearby for self-disposal. We would like to make an exception for people with a low income and the Amstelveen pass.

109238.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen agrees with this. Neighborhood parties in particular can be addressed about this. The nearly 10 festivals in the Amsterdam Forest should be reassessed for noise nuisance to reduce volume and frequency slightly. This would also be better for animals.

109240.explanation: People on social assistance can receive an amount they can dispose of themselves. Further responsibility lies with the beneficiary. They can also use the sports and culture fund.

109243.explanation: Disagree, education is a national policy issue. It is up to the municipality to decide if it wants to contribute to music lessons.

109247.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen values privacy highly and also questions the origin of cameras. Cameras may only be placed where there is a real danger.

109249.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen has no opinion on this but leaves it to experts and the assessment of necessity.

109255.explanation: People on social assistance are not on it without reason. If they can do socially useful work, it is a choice. Goed voor Amstelveen opposes coercion and pressure.

109256.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen knows that municipal taxes are already remitted in certain cases but should not become an absolute rule.

109259.explanation: Amstelveen conducted a basic income pilot that concluded positively. It concerns people who had long been unable to find work and did not feel pressured to work. This made them healthier and more active.

109260.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen believes that acting prematurely is unwise due to network congestion. We believe that for now there are enough charging stations.

109265.explanation: Not all neighborhoods require a parking permit where free parking is possible. Where residents face paid parking on the street and have only one permit and/or a visitor pass, more permits could be issued.

109266.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen agrees with this. The question is whether there are sufficient options to provide the familiar care. A suitable care provider if possible. The reality is more stubborn, and not every health insurer can provide that care.

109271.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen believes Amstelveen has enough business parks and sees future development in the Amstelveen South Business Park.

109279.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen opposes a new coffeeshop; there is already one on the Amsterdam border. Moreover, we are in favor of not encouraging drug use in any form.

109280.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen considers 50% impossible but sees 30% as a possibility. Amstelveen has too little land position to make this a reality. Developers cannot build because it is not profitable.

109281.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen believes the established high-rise vision provides sufficient opportunities and is in principle against high-rise over 9 floors. We want to keep Amstelveen spatially open and see density increasing.

109282.explanation: Landlords are needed to offer housing to people who need it; they should determine this themselves. The rooming house regulation still exists.

109284.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen takes a neutral stance here. Depending on the neighborhood and location, paid parking can be cheaper, or due to pressure on facilities, expansion of parking spaces can be considered without harming green spaces and therefore does not need to be more expensive.

109286.explanation: We consider making the center of Amstelveen car-free to be risky and do not support it. It is not feasible regarding businesses and accessibility for people with disabilities, the elderly, and strollers.

109287.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen supports a warning first and then a fine. The bus sluices with warning signs have been in the news more than once. We assume that people in Amstelveen know they shouldn’t take that risk.

109288.explanation: Goed voor Amstelveen has spoken out more than once in favor of reducing the number of flights. As a municipality with limited influence, we hope to get more support from The Hague.

109289.explanation: People who have problems with the energy bill did not ask for all the energy measures.

statements.theme: Replace fines for passing bus gates with a warning
statements.title: Motorists and motorcyclists who illegally go through the bus gates in Westwijk and Bovenkerk should first receive a warning and only then a fine.
The residents of Amstelveen should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Amstelveen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The center of Amstelveen should become car-free.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
There should be more camera surveillance in Amstelveen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
Amstelveen should provide shelter to rejected asylum seekers.

titleParts.text: Amstelveen moet opvang bieden aan afgewezen

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

statements.title: Amstelveen should introduce a parking permit in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Amstelveen moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Amstelveen should press the national government to reduce the number of airplanes flying over the municipality.
The BOAs who enforce safety in Amstelveen should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Amstelveen controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
In Amstelveen the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Amstelveen moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Amstelveen should run a trial of a basic income.

titleParts.text: Amstelveen moet een proef doen met

titleParts.glossary: With a basic income, people receive a fixed amount per month from the government. They do not have to apply for jobs or perform socially useful work in return for the money.

statements.title: More tall residential towers should be allowed in Amstelveen.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a new coffeeshop (cannabis coffee shop).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: At least 50% of the new homes to be built in Amstelveen should be social housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Amstelveen moet minimaal 50% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Business parks in Amstelveen should be allowed to expand.
Paid parking in the neighborhoods should be made cheaper.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.

statements.theme: More funding to address energy poverty
statements.title: People who struggle to pay their energy bills should receive more financial support from the municipality.
